intro: Elachyptera floribunda, also known as the Lemon Myrtle, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its highly aromatic leaves and vibrant yellow flowers. The leaves of the Lemon Myrtle plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, fever, and skin irritations, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elachyptera floribunda include citral, limonene, and geranial, which are responsible for its antiseptic, antiviral, and antifungal activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are commonly used to aid digestion, reduce stress, and promote a sense of calmness and relaxation.
